data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn mk(a: A): produce OptionA / {} := OptionA.some(a)
---
mk : (A) -> produce OptionA / {}
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn ok_mk(a: A): produce OptionA / {} := produce OptionA.some(a)
---
ok_mk : (A) -> produce OptionA / {}
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badp1(a: OptionA, b: A): produce A / {} := match a { some(x) => produce b, .none => produce b }
---
ERROR: constructor patterns must start with `.`
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badp2(a: OptionA, b: A): produce A / {} := match a { some => produce b, .none => produce b }
---
ERROR: variant pattern `some` must be written `.some`
==========
data Nat { .zero, .succ(Nat) }
data Option[A] { .some(A), .none }
fn sub1(x: Nat): produce Option[Nat] / {} := match x { .zero => Option.none, .succ(let x) => Option.some(x) }
---
sub1 : (Nat) -> produce Option[Nat] / {}
==========
data Nat { .zero, .succ(Nat) }
data Option[A] { .some(A), .none }
fn get_or_zero(o: Option[Nat], z: Nat): produce Nat / {} := match o { .some(x) => produce x, .none => produce z }
---
get_or_zero : (Option[Nat], Nat) -> produce Nat / {}
==========
data Option[A] { .some(A), .none }
fn ok_nil(u: Unit): produce Unit / {} := let x = Option.none in produce u
---
ok_nil : (Unit) -> produce Unit / {}
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn not(x: Bool): produce Bool / {} := match x { .true => Bool.false, .false => Bool.true }
---
not : (Bool) -> produce Bool / {}
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn not(x: Bool): produce Bool / {} := match x { .true => Bool.false(), .false => Bool.true() }
---
ERROR: which is not a function
